fact: The Detroit Lions won the NFL Championship, defeating the Cleveland Browns 59-14 at Briggs Stadium.

The Detroit Gems of the NBL (forerunner to the NBA) relocated to Minneapolis and became the Lakers.
The Fort Wayne Pistons relocated to Detroit.
The Detroit Pistons played at Cobo Arena before moving to the Pontiac Silverdome.
The Pistons moved from the Pontiac Silverdome to The Palace of Auburn Hills.
Kirk Gibson, former Tiger, hit one of the most famous home runs in World Series history — a walk-off homer for the Dodgers in Game 1 of the 1988 Series.
The Detroit Lions selected wide receiver Roy Williams 7th overall in the 2004 Draft.
Herman Moore, the Lions' all-time leading receiver at the time, played his final game for Detroit.
The Lions' Thanksgiving Day classic: the 1998 game in which Jason Hanson kicked a game-winning field goal to beat the Steelers 19-16 in OT.
The Lions set an NFL record by rallying from a 21-3 halftime deficit to defeat the Bears 38-31 on Thanksgiving.
Matthew Stafford threw for 5,038 yards in 2011, becoming just the fourth QB to throw for 5,000 yards in a season.
Matthew Stafford led the Lions to the playoffs for the first time since 1999 with an 11-5 record.
The Lions defeated the Bears 37-23 on Monday Night Football behind Matthew Stafford's heroic comeback despite a separated shoulder.
The 'Butt Fumble' — Mark Sanchez's infamous play — happened in a Jets loss to the Patriots, not a Michigan game, but Michigan's Denard Robinson won Big Ten Offensive Player of the Year that season.
Michigan quarterback Denard Robinson set an NCAA record for rushing yards by a quarterback with 1,702 yards.
Michigan basketball's Trey Burke hit a game-tying three-pointer against Kansas in the Sweet 16, later called 'The Shot.'
Trey Burke won the Wooden Award as college basketball's best player.
Michigan basketball upset #2 Purdue in overtime at Crisler Center.
Tom Izzo recorded his 600th career win as Michigan State head coach.
The Detroit Lions franchise's first game (as the Portsmouth Spartans) was played.
The Pistons lost the 2007 Eastern Conference Finals to LeBron James and the Cleveland Cavaliers, who won the last 4 games including a legendary 48-point Game 5 performance by LeBron.
LeBron James scored 29 of the Cavaliers' final 30 points (including 25 straight) to eliminate the Pistons in the 2007 ECF Game 5.
The Palace of Auburn Hills was demolished.
Ben Wallace's number 3 was retired by the Detroit Pistons.
Chauncey Billups's number 1 was retired by the Detroit Pistons.
The Red Wings held a public farewell ceremony at Joe Louis Arena with former players skating on the ice one final time.
The Red Wings signed free agent goaltender Dominik Hasek, who helped them win the 2002 Stanley Cup.
Chris Osgood won his 400th career NHL game as a Red Wing.
The Red Wings acquired Marian Hossa, who scored key playoff goals in their 2008 Cup run.
The Red Wings acquired Brett Hull, adding to their star-studded 2002 championship roster.
The 2002 Red Wings roster featured 9 future Hockey Hall of Famers, often called the greatest team ever assembled.
Luc Robitaille scored his 300th goal as a Red Wing.
Jimmy Howard was selected in the 2nd round by the Red Wings and became their starting goaltender for over a decade.
The Red Wings lost the 2009 Stanley Cup Finals to the Pittsburgh Penguins in 7 games.
Western Michigan's Greg Jennings was drafted by the Green Bay Packers and became a Super Bowl champion.
Central Michigan's Dan LeFevour became the first player in NCAA history to pass for 12,000 yards and rush for 2,500.
Eric Ebron was drafted 10th overall by the Detroit Lions out of the University of North Carolina.
The Michigan Panthers revived as a USFL team in the 2022 reboot of the league.
The 2005 MLB All-Star Game was held at Comerica Park in Detroit.
The 2012 MLB All-Star Game was hosted at Kauffman Stadium but featured Triple Crown winner Miguel Cabrera of the Tigers as the starting first baseman.
The 1971 MLB All-Star Game was held at Tiger Stadium. Reggie Jackson hit a memorable home run off the light tower.
The 1941 MLB All-Star Game was held at Briggs Stadium (Tiger Stadium) in Detroit.
The 1951 MLB All-Star Game was held at Briggs Stadium in Detroit.
The NHL All-Star Game was held at Joe Louis Arena in Detroit.
The 1954 NBA All-Star Game was the first held in a neutral site arena — at Madison Square Garden, but featured Pistons from Fort Wayne.
The 2003 NBA All-Star Game was held at Philips Arena but the Pistons' Ben Wallace was an All-Star starter.
Michigan Wolverines women's softball won the NCAA Championship, defeating UCLA.
The Detroit Tigers hosted their first game at Bennett Park, the predecessor to Tiger Stadium.
The Detroit Tigers won the American League pennant for the first time.
Ty Cobb won the first of his 12 batting titles.
Ty Cobb hit .420 in 1911, one of the highest single-season batting averages in modern baseball history.
The Detroit Tigers won their first World Series in 1935, defeating the Chicago Cubs.
Charlie Gehringer won the AL MVP award for the Tigers.
Virgil Trucks threw two no-hitters in a single season for the Tigers, despite the team losing 104 games.
Jim Bunning threw a no-hitter for the Tigers against the Red Sox.
The Detroit Express of the NASL (North American Soccer League) played at the Pontiac Silverdome.
The Pontiac Silverdome hosted the 1994 FIFA World Cup matches.
The Red Wings acquired Dino Ciccarelli in a trade with the Washington Capitals.
The Red Wings' Grind Line of Kris Draper, Kirk Maltby, Darren McCarty, and Joe Kocur became fan favorites.
The Hockeytown nickname was officially trademarked by the Detroit Red Wings.
The Joe Louis Arena demolition was completed.
Little Caesars Arena hosted its first Red Wings game.
The Lions selected Jahmyr Gibbs 12th overall in the 2023 NFL Draft.
The Lions selected Jameson Williams 12th overall in the 2022 NFL Draft.
The Lions selected Penei Sewell 7th overall in the 2021 NFL Draft.
The Lions won their first Thanksgiving Day game under Dan Campbell, defeating the Bears 16-14.
Dan Campbell's emotional 'bite kneecaps' introductory press conference went viral.
The Lions defeated the Kansas City Chiefs 21-20 in the NFL season opener, a statement win.
Michigan's Jake Long was the 1st overall pick in the 2008 NFL Draft by the Miami Dolphins.
Michigan's Rashan Gary was selected 12th overall by the Green Bay Packers.
Michigan's Chase Winovich, Devin Bush, and Rashan Gary were all selected in the first two rounds of the 2019 NFL Draft.
Michigan State's Darqueze Dennard was selected 24th overall, and the Spartans had multiple first-rounders in the 2014 NFL Draft.
Michigan's Tom Brady, the greatest quarterback of all time, won his 7th Super Bowl — more than any franchise in NFL history.
The Michigan-Ohio State rivalry, known as 'The Game,' is considered the greatest rivalry in all of sports. The first meeting was in 1897.
Michigan's famous winged helmet was introduced by coach Fritz Crisler.
The Ten Year War between Bo Schembechler and Woody Hayes began, defined by intense Michigan-Ohio State matchups from 1969-1978.
Fielding Yost's 'Point-a-Minute' teams went 56-1-1 from 1901-1905 at Michigan.
Michigan won the first Rose Bowl game ever played, defeating Stanford 49-0.
Gerald Ford played center for the Michigan Wolverines football team and was offered NFL contracts.
The University of Michigan's athletic department adopted the 'Go Blue' slogan.
Michigan Stadium was renovated, adding permanent lighting for night games and luxury boxes.
The Detroit Lions' Thanksgiving game against the Houston Texans drew 32.2 million viewers in 2023, the most-watched NFL regular season game in history.
The Red Wings signed Alex DeBrincat in free agency.
The Red Wings traded for goaltender Ville Husso from the St. Louis Blues.
Pavel Datsyuk won his third consecutive Selke Trophy as the NHL's best defensive forward.
Pavel Datsyuk won the Lady Byng Trophy for the fourth consecutive time.
The Red Wings drafted Henrik Zetterberg in the 7th round, 210th overall — one of the greatest late-round steals in NHL history.
The Red Wings drafted Pavel Datsyuk in the 6th round, 171st overall — another legendary late-round pick.
Gordie Howe returned to professional hockey at age 45, joining the WHA's Houston Aeros to play with his sons Mark and Marty.
Gordie Howe played his final NHL game at age 52 for the Hartford Whalers.
The first Thanksgiving Day football game in Detroit was held at the University of Detroit.
Michigan hosted the 'Big Chill at the Big House' — an outdoor hockey game that drew 104,173 fans, setting a world record.
The Pistons' 2004 championship team was the first NBA champion without a single player averaging 16+ points per game.
Isiah Thomas retired as a Detroit Piston after 13 seasons.
The Bad Boys 30 for 30 documentary premiered on ESPN.
Dave DeBusschere became the youngest player-coach in NBA history at age 24 with the Detroit Pistons.
Rasheed Wallace set the NBA record for technical fouls in a season with 41 (before joining the Pistons).
Grand Valley State Lakers won the Division II football national championship for the sixth time overall.
Saginaw Valley State reached the Division II Final Four in basketball.
The Michigan Wolverines beat Ohio State in Ann Arbor 42-39 in one of the highest-scoring editions of 'The Game.'
Michigan State's Connor Cook was drafted in the 4th round by the Oakland Raiders.
The Detroit City FC joined the USL Championship, bringing professional outdoor soccer back to Detroit.
Michigan basketball's Phil Hubbard was the first African American player to start for the Wolverines and was drafted 15th overall by the Cavaliers.
Michigan's Cazzie Russell was the #1 overall pick in the 1966 NBA Draft.
Michigan basketball won back-to-back Big Ten titles under John Beilein.
John Beilein resigned as Michigan basketball coach to take the Cleveland Cavaliers job.
Michigan basketball reached the Sweet 16 under Dusty May in his first season.
Dusty May was hired as Michigan basketball's head coach.
Michigan State basketball made an Elite Eight run under Tom Izzo, defeating Duke in the 2019 Regional Final.
Michigan gymnastics has been a perennial top-10 program, producing numerous Olympic gymnasts.
The Detroit Red Wings' Chris Chelios played until age 48, one of the oldest players in NHL history.
The Red Wings traded for defenseman Jeff Petry from the Edmonton Oilers.
The Tigers' Mark 'The Bird' Fidrych tragically died in an accident at age 54.
The Tigers selected Spencer Torkelson 1st overall in the 2020 MLB Draft.
The Tigers selected Riley Greene 5th overall in the 2019 MLB Draft.
The Tigers hired A.J. Hinch as manager.
The Tigers acquired Javier Báez on a 6-year, $140 million contract.
The Tigers' Willie Hernandez won both the Cy Young Award and MVP in 1984.
The Detroit Lions won their first playoff game in 32 years, defeating the Dallas Cowboys 34-9 in the Wild Card round under Dan Campbell.
The Detroit Lions lost the 2025 NFC Divisional Round to the Washington Commanders 45-31.
The Lions went 15-2 in the 2024 regular season, the best record in the NFL and franchise history.
Aidan Hutchinson suffered a devastating broken leg on national TV against the Dallas Cowboys.
The Detroit Tigers clinched a playoff berth in 2024, their first postseason appearance since 2014.
The Tigers upset the Houston Astros in the 2024 ALDS Wild Card series.
The Tigers were eliminated by the Cleveland Guardians in the 2024 ALDS.
Tarik Skubal won the AL Cy Young Award for the 2024 season with the Tigers.
The Lions drafted running back D'Andre Swift in the 2nd round of the 2020 NFL Draft out of Georgia.
The Lions' David Montgomery and Jahmyr Gibbs formed one of the most effective running back duos in the NFL in 2023.
Sam LaPorta set a Lions rookie record for receptions by a tight end in 2023.
The Lions selected Sam LaPorta in the 2nd round of the 2023 Draft out of Iowa.
Michigan State hired Tom Izzo's former assistant assistant coach. Izzo has coached in East Lansing for over 28 seasons.
Michigan's 'Victors' fight song, written in 1898, is considered one of the greatest fight songs in college sports.
The first night game at Spartan Stadium was played by Michigan State.
Central Michigan's Rasheen Mathis was drafted in the 2nd round by the Jacksonville Jaguars and had a 14-year NFL career.
Western Michigan reached the MAC Championship game multiple times, including 2016 when they went undefeated in the regular season.
Ferris State won the NCAA Division II football national championship.
Grand Valley State's Brian Kelly coached the Lakers to two Division II national titles before moving to Central Michigan, Cincinnati, Notre Dame, and LSU.
The 1997 Michigan football team was coached by Lloyd Carr and featured Heisman winner Charles Woodson, All-American DB.
Michigan defensive end LaMarr Woodley was drafted 46th overall by the Pittsburgh Steelers and won Super Bowl XLIII.
